name: Pate Aux Poireaux (Leek Tart)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup White cheddar cheese, mild - grated
recipeInstructions:
Pate aux Poireaux
This leek and cheese quiche from Ile d'Orleans is related to the Flamiche aux poireuax, a savory leek tart made in northern France and Flanders. The same recipe can also be used to make small tarts.
1. Heat 4 tablespoons of the butter in a heavy saucepan. Add the chopped leeks and cook over low heat for 10 minutes. Stir occasionally.
2. Add water and cover the pan. Simmer gently for 20 to 25 minutes, or until the leeks are tender.
3. In another saucepan, melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter. Gradually blend in the flour, cooking over medium heat until bubbling.
4. Blend in the leek mixture into the flour mixture, stirring constantly until it comes to a boil. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Beat the egg and cream together. Mix this with the leek sauce and pour into an unbaked tart shell.
6. Sprinkle the top evenly with grated cheddar cheese.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F (200°C) for 25 to 30 minutes, or until the cheese begins to brown. Serve at room temperature, warmed through.
SERVES: 6
